sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Retail investors account for 43% of Smart Sand, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:SND) ownership, while institutions account for 21%
feed.summary: Retail investors hold the largest share of Smart Sand, Inc. (NASDAQ:SND) at 43%, giving them significant potential influence over management decisions, while institutions own 21% and private companies hold 13%. Insiders also own a reasonable portion of the company, valued at US$28 million, indicating alignment with shareholder interests. The top 19 shareholders collectively control 51% of the ownership, but no single shareholder has a majority interest.

feed.title: Research Frontiers Reports Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results and Will Host a Conference Call at 4:30p.m. Today
feed.summary: Research Frontiers Inc. (Nasdaq: REFR) announced its third-quarter 2025 financial results, highlighting growth in its SPD-SmartGlass technology across various markets, including a new retrofit system for architectural use and increased automotive royalty income. The company remains debt-free with $1.13 million in cash and will host a conference call today at 4:30 PM ET to discuss these results and recent developments. President and CEO Joseph M. Harary noted the technology's increasing market traction and global recognition.

feed.title: UBS Group Raises International Flavors & Fragrances (NYSE:IFF) Price Target to $71.00
feed.summary: UBS Group has increased its price target for International Flavors & Fragrances (NYSE:IFF) to $71.00 from $68.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating. This new target suggests a potential upside of 2.99% from the stock's current price, though the consensus target among analysts is significantly higher at $85.19, indicating a "Moderate Buy". The company recently beat quarterly EPS estimates, reporting $1.05 versus $1.02, on revenues of $2.69 billion, despite a 7.9% year-over-year revenue decline and a negative net margin.

feed.title: Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG) Price Target Raised to $235.00 at UBS Group
feed.summary: UBS Group has raised its price target for Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG) to $235.00 from $230.00, while maintaining a "neutral" rating on the stock. This new target suggests an 8.3% potential upside from the current trading price. The company recently reported quarterly earnings of $2.73 per share, missing estimates slightly, with revenue up 6% to $2.31 billion, and has provided Q4 2025 EPS guidance of approximately $2.40.

feed.title: Con Edison proposes 3-year electricity and gas rate hikes by more than 10 percent by 2028
feed.summary: Con Edison has proposed a three-year rate increase for electricity and gas, with electricity prices potentially rising over 12% and gas prices over 10% by 2028. This proposal has met strong public and political opposition in Westchester County, where local leaders argue the hikes exceed inflation and would create significant financial burdens for residents and businesses. A ConEd spokesperson emphasized the company's commitment to balancing investments for reliability with customer costs and community support.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs resumes Arcus Biosciences stock coverage with Buy rating By Investing.com
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has reinitiated coverage on Arcus Biosciences (NYSE: RCUS) with a Buy rating and a raised price target of $28.00, citing the potential of its lead asset casdatifan, a HIF-2α inhibitor for advanced clear cell renal cell carcinoma, which has a projected addressable market of 17,400 patients and risk-adjusted peak sales of $1.7 billion. Despite the company's significant cash burn, impressive Phase 1 results for casdatifan have been noted, and analysts expect catalyst-rich readouts in 2026. This positive outlook contrasts with mixed recent analyst adjustments following strategic financial moves and the discontinuation of a Phase 3 study.

feed.title: Moody’s Corp. Elects Lisa Sawicki to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Moody's Corporation (NYSE:MCO) has announced the election of Lisa P. Sawicki to its Board of Directors, effective March 16, 2026. Ms. Sawicki brings over 35 years of experience in financial services audit and advisory, previously serving as Chair of the Global Board at PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP. Her expertise is expected to help Moody's navigate a complex global environment and capitalize on future opportunities.

feed.title: ‘People over profits’: State lawmakers rally against Con Edison rate hikes
feed.summary: New York state lawmakers, joined by city council members, rallied against Con Edison's proposed gas and electricity rate hikes, urging the Public Service Commission to reject the increase. They argue that the proposed 12% electricity and 4.4% gas rate increases, totaling about $17 per month for the average household, would burden New Yorkers already struggling with affordability. Con Edison asserts the increase is necessary due to rising costs and highlights its affordability programs and flexible payment options.

feed.title: Applied Materials Stock: Riding The AI Hardware Wave While Valuations Get Tested
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has become a crucial supplier to the AI and advanced chip industry, experiencing significant stock appreciation despite recent volatility. Analysts are largely constructive on the stock, with most maintaining "Buy" equivalent ratings and expecting further upside. The company's future performance hinges on AI-related capital spending, the broader semiconductor cycle, and geopolitical factors, with the market cautiously optimistic about its central role in the ecosystem.

feed.title: General Dynamics (GD) Secures $988M U.S. Navy Contract
feed.summary: General Dynamics' IT division (GDIT) has been awarded a $988 million contract by the U.S. Navy to enhance its C5ISR systems. This significant contract, which includes options for extension, led to a slight increase in General Dynamics' shares in after-hours trading. The defense contractor, known for its diverse operations in aerospace, marine, combat systems, and technologies, shows strong financial health despite some declining margins, and is considered moderately overvalued by current valuation metrics.

feed.title: JPM26: US biotech’s ‘Sputnik moment,’ Pfizer’s obesity ambitions and Bristol Myers’ big year
feed.summary: The annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference highlighted a transforming biotech landscape marked by increased M&A activity and the growing influence of Chinese biotechs, prompting some to call for a "Sputnik moment" for the US industry. Major pharmaceutical companies like Bristol Myers Squibb and Pfizer are strategizing to offset patent expirations and boost future growth, with Pfizer making a significant bet on obesity drugs. Meanwhile, companies such as Sarepta Therapeutics face scrutiny after failed drug trials and revised sales forecasts, reflecting the volatile nature of the industry for both established players and emerging biotechs.

feed.title: General Motors CEO Marry Barra on the company right now
feed.summary: General Motors CEO Mary Barra discussed the company with Automotive Press Association president Kalae Hall in Detroit. The article mentions this event alongside various other video clips related to sports, local news, and other Detroit-area topics.

feed.title: DTE’s fast-tracked data center contracts need second look, Nessel says
feed.summary: Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel has requested a rehearing on the state's fast-track approval of DTE Energy's contracts to supply power to an Oracle data center. Nessel and environmental/consumer groups argue that the Michigan Public Service Commission's ex parte approval process, which prevented outside intervention and limited transparency through heavy redactions, does not adequately protect consumers from potential energy rate increases. While the Commission states its order includes strong consumer protections, Nessel questions whether DTE will pass costs to customers, and advocacy groups also expressed concerns about the enforcement of contract conditions.

feed.title: Verisign EVP Indelicarto sells $41,500 in shares By Investing.com
feed.summary: Verisign's EVP, General Counsel, and Secretary, Thomas C. Indelicarto, sold 166 shares of common stock for $41,500 on January 9, 2026. This transaction occurred despite Verisign's ongoing share repurchase program and strong financial health. The company, valued at $23 billion, is scheduled to release its earnings report on February 5, 2026.

feed.title: Abbott Laboratories Stock: Quiet Grind Higher as Wall Street Warms to the Recovery Story
feed.summary: Abbott Laboratories stock has been steadily climbing, driven by solid operating performance, easing diagnostics headwinds, and an improving macro environment for medical devices. Recent analyst upgrades and positive commentary on its innovation pipeline across cardiovascular, diabetes care, and nutrition segments are contributing to a constructive outlook. Despite rising valuations, the company is seen as a quality compounder poised for continued growth, rewarding patient investors.

feed.title: FFIV: Kirby McInerney LLP Advises F5, Inc. Investors of Class Action Lawsuit
feed.summary: Kirby McInerney LLP is informing investors of F5, Inc. (NASDAQ:FFIV) about a class action lawsuit alleging securities fraud. The lawsuit claims F5 made misleading statements about its cybersecurity capabilities while experiencing a significant breach, which eventually led to a stock price drop after disclosure. Investors who suffered losses have until February 17, 2026, to seek lead plaintiff appointment.

feed.title: Revelation Biosciences Reports Inducement Grant Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4)
feed.summary: Revelation Biosciences, Inc. (NASDAQ:REVB) announced an inducement grant of 90,000 restricted stock units (RSUs) to a new non-executive employee on January 8, 2026. This grant was made outside the Company's stockholder-approved equity incentive plan, as a material inducement for the employee's acceptance of employment, in accordance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4). The RSUs will vest over two years in equal quarterly installments, contingent on the employee's continued service.

feed.title: Costco stock rises as insider sale filing hits tape; key company dates ahead for COST
feed.summary: Costco's stock rose nearly 2% after a regulatory filing showed Senior Executive Vice President Russell D. Miller sold 1,500 shares for $1.37 million. The company released strong December sales figures, and analysts like Mizuho remain upbeat, raising price targets. Upcoming key dates for investors include the annual shareholder meeting, January sales figures, and fiscal second-quarter earnings.

feed.title: Sky Quarry Establishes At-the-Market Equity Offering Program
feed.summary: Sky Quarry Inc. has established an at-the-market (ATM) equity offering program through a sales agreement with Cantor Fitzgerald & Co., allowing it to sell up to $4.7 million in common stock. This program provides the company with flexible capital-raising capacity, although it carries potential for dilution for existing shareholders. Currently, analysts rate SKYQ as a Hold with a $0.59 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, assigns a "Neutral" score due to poor financial performance and weak technicals.

feed.title: FINAL DEADLINE REMINDER: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Reminds Freeport-McMoran Investors of the Pending Class Action Lawsuit with a Lead Plaintiff Deadline of January 12, 2026
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is reminding investors of Freeport-McMoran Inc. (NYSE: FCX) about the upcoming January 12, 2026 lead plaintiff deadline for a federal securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Freeport-McMoran made false and misleading statements by failing to disclose inadequate safety measures at its Grasberg Block Cave mine in Indonesia, which led to worker deaths and subsequent drops in stock price. Investors affected by the alleged misrepresentations are encouraged to contact the law firm to learn more about the lawsuit and their options.

feed.title: MBX Biosciences (NASDAQ:MBX) Trading 7.6% Higher - Time to Buy?
feed.summary: MBX Biosciences (NASDAQ:MBX) saw its stock rise 7.6% on Monday, trading on light volume. The company has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $47.22, and both the CEO and a director recently purchased shares. MBX also reported an earnings beat in its last quarter.

feed.title: UBS Maintains 'Buy' Rating for Albemarle (ALB), Increases Price Target | ALB Stock News
feed.summary: UBS has reaffirmed its 'Buy' rating for Albemarle (ALB) and increased its price target to $205 from $185, signaling strong confidence in the company's future performance. This adjustment follows similar positive revisions from other analysts, highlighting Albemarle's strategic market position as a leading lithium producer. Despite an average target price from 23 analysts suggesting a potential downside, the consensus brokerage recommendation for ALB remains "Outperform."

feed.title: Mosaic (MOS) Price Target Lowered by UBS to $33, Maintains Buy R
feed.summary: UBS analyst Lucas Beaumont has lowered the price target for Mosaic (MOS) to $33 from $38, while maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment reflects ongoing market evaluations, following similar price target reductions by other firms like Mizuho, Barclays, JP Morgan, and Goldman Sachs in recent months. Despite the lowered target, the average analyst forecast still implies an upside of 27.19% from the current price, with GuruFocus estimating an 18.51% upside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Kratos shares surge as Northrop partnership potentially brings more work to Sacramento
feed.summary: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions Inc. shares have recently soared due to analyst upgrades, a call for increased defense spending from President Trump, and a new partnership with Northrop Grumman Corp. This partnership could bring additional work to the Sacramento area. The article highlights Kratos' involvement in defense manufacturing and its low-cost cruise missile plans.

feed.title: DEFT Investors Have Opportunity to Lead DeFi Technologies, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: The Rosen Law Firm reminds purchasers of DeFi Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: DEFT) securities between May 12, 2025, and November 14, 2025, of an upcoming lead plaintiff deadline on January 30, 2026, for a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that DeFi Technologies made false and misleading statements regarding its DeFi arbitrage strategy, competition, and revenue guidance for fiscal year 2025. Investors who purchased securities during this period may be entitled to compensation and are encouraged to contact the firm.

feed.title: General Dynamics’ unit wins $988 million US Navy contract By Reuters
feed.summary: General Dynamics' information technology unit has secured a $988 million contract from the U.S. Navy to modernize its fleet systems. The contract aims to upgrade command, control, and surveillance technologies and enhance the readiness of naval forces, including guided missile ships, aircraft carriers, and Coast Guard vessels. The initial base period is one year, with options for extensions.

feed.title: F5, Inc. (FFIV) Cybersecurity Incident-Related Securities Class Action Pending As Adverse Financial Impact Clarified - Hagens Berman
feed.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against F5, Inc. (FFIV) following revelations of a "material cybersecurity incident" discovered on August 9, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that F5 may have misled investors regarding the security of its products, the financial impact of the breach, and the timeliness of its disclosures, which led to significant stock price declines. Hagens Berman is investigating these claims, focusing on whether F5 management knew about the incident's materiality long before public disclosure.

feed.title: Coupang, Inc. (CPNG) Class Period Expanded in Pending Investor Securities Lawsuit - Hagens Berman
feed.summary: Hagens Berman has announced an expansion of the class period in a securities class action lawsuit against Coupang, Inc. (CPNG), now covering investors who purchased securities between May 7, 2025, and December 16, 2025. The lawsuit investigates statements made by Coupang regarding its disclosure controls, cybersecurity protocols, and transparency concerning a data breach affecting millions of customer accounts. The firm encourages affected investors to come forward, as the Lead Plaintiff Deadline is February 17, 2026.

feed.title: F5, Inc. (FFIV) Cybersecurity Incident-Related Securities Class Action Pending As Adverse Financial Impact Clarified - Hagens Berman
feed.summary: A securities class action lawsuit is pending against F5, Inc. (FFIV) following a "material cybersecurity incident" discovered on August 9, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that F5 may have misled investors about the security of its products, the incident's financial impact, and the timeliness of its disclosures, which caused significant drops in share price after revelations in October 2025. Hagens Berman is investigating claims that F5 management knew about the materiality of the breach earlier than disclosed.

feed.title: Öl-Service by Halliburton: The Quiet Backbone Powering the World’s Energy Giants
feed.summary: Halliburton's "Öl-Service" provides integrated oilfield services, ranging from drilling and completion to digital subsurface intelligence, aiming to make hydrocarbon projects more predictable and profitable. The article highlights Halliburton's strengths in scale, integration, digitalization, and technical depth, positioning it as a strategic partner for complex operations despite potential drawbacks like cost and bureaucracy. It emphasizes that choosing Halliburton means investing in an ecosystem designed to reduce operational uncertainty and optimize performance across the entire well lifecycle.

feed.title: Microsoft, Oracle and ServiceNow could be the top stocks to play a software comeback
feed.summary: Analysts from Goldman Sachs and Deutsche Bank suggest that infrastructure software companies, specifically Microsoft, Oracle, and ServiceNow, are poised for significant gains in 2026. This sector is expected to benefit greatly from the increasing adoption of AI, as these companies manage the hardware and data essential for AI infrastructure. Goldman Sachs projects that AI could expand the total addressable market for the software sector by 30% by 2037.

feed.title: General Dynamics' unit wins $988 million US Navy contract
feed.summary: General Dynamics' unit has secured a significant $988 million contract from the US Navy. This news highlights a major defense deal involving the company. The brief news item indicates a substantial financial award for General Dynamics.

feed.title: Oracle stock jumps after Goldman’s Buy call as AI buildout costs stay in focus
feed.summary: Oracle shares saw a significant jump of roughly 3% in after-hours trading following Goldman Sachs' initiation of coverage with a Buy rating and a $240 price target. The positive outlook is driven by the expanding software market due to AI, although Goldman Sachs analyst Gabriela Borges emphasized the challenge for companies like Oracle to convert AI infrastructure demand into sustainable profits, particularly given the high data center and energy costs associated with AI workloads. Oracle also announced new product updates, including a retail supply-chain cloud tool and a new hospital client for Oracle Health, further positioning itself in the evolving AI and cloud landscape.

feed.title: Bham Fortune 500 company names new CFO
feed.summary: Regions Financial Corporation has announced that CFO David Turner will retire on March 31, and Anil Chadha will succeed him. Chadha's appointment follows Turner's retirement, marking a significant executive transition for the Birmingham-based Fortune 500 company. This change will be effective following Turner's departure.

feed.title: Western Union (WU) Target Lifted at Keefe Bruyette in Payments Sector Update
feed.summary: Keefe Bruyette raised its price target on Western Union (WU) to $10 from $9, maintaining a Market Perform rating, as part of a broader update in the consumer finance and payments sector. The company is actively pursuing a digital transformation, expanding its travel money business, and exploring stablecoin technology, including plans to launch a USDPT on Solana in the first half of 2026. This initiative aims to broaden money movement across its network and enhance treasury flexibility.

feed.title: Revelation Biosciences Reports Inducement Grant Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4)
feed.summary: Revelation Biosciences, Inc. (NASDAQ:REVB), a clinical-stage life sciences company, announced an inducement grant of 90,000 restricted stock units (RSUs) to a new non-executive employee. This grant, approved by the Compensation Committee, was made outside of the Company's stockholder-approved equity incentive plan to induce the employee's acceptance of employment, in accordance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4). The RSUs will vest quarterly over two years, contingent on continued service.

feed.title: Biotech recruits new employee with 90,000 stock units over 2 years
feed.summary: Revelation Biosciences (NASDAQ: REVB) announced an inducement equity grant of 90,000 restricted stock units (RSUs) to a new non-executive employee on January 8, 2026. This grant was made outside the company's stockholder-approved equity incentive plan under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4) as an inducement for employment. The RSUs will vest over two years in equal quarterly installments, contingent on the employee's continued service.

feed.title: Else Nutrition Announces Results from Annual General Meeting of Shareholders
feed.summary: Else Nutrition Holdings Inc. (TSX: BABY) announced that all resolutions were passed at its annual general meeting of shareholders held on December 29, 2025. Shareholders re-elected five directors, re-appointed Kost Forer Gabbay & Kassierer as the company's auditor, and approved the issuance of common shares to Lind Global Fund II, LP and Lind Global Fund III, LP, as well as the continuation of the stock option plan. The meeting, which took place in Vancouver, BC, had 17.30% of the company's shares represented.

feed.title: National Storage Affiliates (NSA) Target Trimmed at UBS as REITs Head Into 2026 Reset
feed.summary: UBS has lowered its price target for National Storage Affiliates (NYSE: NSA) to $29 from $30, maintaining a Neutral rating. This adjustment reflects UBS's broader outlook on REITs, which anticipates a 9-11% total return in 2026 driven by improving macro conditions and easing supply pressures. National Storage Affiliates is also expanding through a new joint venture with Investment Real Estate Management, focusing on value-add self-storage opportunities.

feed.title: QXO announces additional $1.8 billion raise for deal war chest
feed.summary: QXO has secured an additional $1.8 billion in funding from Apollo, Temasek, and other investors, bringing its total war chest for acquisitions to $3 billion. The company, founded by Brad Jacobs, aims to disrupt the building products industry and increase its annual revenue from $10 billion to $50 billion within five years. QXO is in late-stage discussions with seven potential acquisition targets, both private and family-owned businesses in the US and Europe, with a deadline to announce a deal by July 15.

feed.title: Waters Corp Announces Presentation at J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference
feed.summary: Waters Corp (WAT) announced its participation in the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference on January 12, 2026. During the presentation, the company discussed its business performance, future outlook, and strategic initiatives, including a proposed transaction with Becton, Dickinson and SpinCo. The presentation materials are available on Waters Corp's website and an SEC 8-K filing provides further details.

feed.title: Regions Financial announces David Turner to retire, Anil Chadha to serve as Chief Financial Officer
feed.summary: Regions Financial Corporation announced that Chief Financial Officer David Turner will retire on March 31 after 20 years with the bank. Anil Chadha, currently Controller and head of Corporate Finance, will succeed Turner as CFO. Turner is credited with skillfully leading the company through a challenging period for the banking industry, while Chadha brings extensive experience in finance and risk management to his new role.

feed.title: Alliant Energy Corporation Declares Quarterly Common Stock Dividend
feed.summary: Alliant Energy Corporation has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.5350 per share, payable on February 17, 2026, to shareowners of record as of January 30, 2026. The company has consistently paid dividends for 321 consecutive quarters since 1946 and is recognized as a member of the S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats Index. Alliant Energy provides regulated energy services to approximately 1 million electric and 430,000 natural gas customers across Iowa and Wisconsin.

feed.title: Why Capital One (COF) Stock Is Trading Lower Today
feed.summary: Capital One (COF) stock fell 6.6% after President Donald Trump announced a plan to implement a one-year, 10% cap on credit card interest rates. This proposal is significantly lower than the average commercial bank interest rate of nearly 21%, causing concerns about the profitability of credit card lenders and their ability to extend credit. Industry groups have voiced opposition, suggesting the cap could reduce credit availability in the market.

feed.title: Sleep Number Corp. Introduces ComfortMode Mattress
feed.summary: Sleep Number Corporation has launched its new ComfortMode mattress, designed to be its most comfortable option at a sub-$1,600 price point, requiring no app or connectivity. This new offering, along with an accompanying adjustable base priced under $3,000, aims to attract a broader consumer base to the Sleep Number brand. The ComfortMode mattress features enhanced comfort layers, firmness adjustability, and temperature balancing, providing a luxurious feel at an accessible price.

feed.title: Scotiabank trims Verizon (VZ) price target as wireless promotions intensify
feed.summary: Scotiabank reduced its price target for Verizon (VZ) from $45 to $43, maintaining a Sector Perform rating. This adjustment was made due to an anticipated increase in promotional intensity within the wireless sector, indicating potential challenges for Verizon's market performance.

feed.title: Cadence Design Systems Insider Sold Shares Worth $2,009,189, According to a Recent SEC Filing
feed.summary: An insider at Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) recently sold shares valued at $2,009,189, as reported in a new SEC filing. This transaction comes amidst various other insider sales and company news, including Cadence launching a partner ecosystem and analyst price target adjustments. The company specializes in developing design software for integrated circuits and electronic systems.

feed.title: Miller, Costco SVP sells $1.37 million in COST stock
feed.summary: Russell D. Miller, Senior Executive Vice President at Costco Wholesale, sold 1,500 shares of Costco stock worth $1.37 million on January 9, 2026. Despite the insider selling, Costco's financial health is rated "GREAT" by InvestingPro, and the company has shown strong sales growth in recent reports, leading to positive analyst ratings.

feed.title: UBS Likes Pfizer's MTSR Obesity Deal but Stays Neutral on PFE
feed.summary: UBS initiated coverage on Pfizer Inc. (NYSE:PFE) with a Neutral rating and a $25 price target, citing uncertainty around future revenue due to upcoming patent expirations, despite liking the recent MTSR obesity deal. The firm suggests Pfizer needs more strategic moves to address long-term risks. Additionally, Pfizer announced a new AI-focused partnership with Boltz, PBC, to enhance drug discovery and development processes.

feed.title: AbbVie Becomes Latest To License PD-1/VEGF Bispecific In Growing Field
feed.summary: AbbVie has announced a licensing deal with RemeGen for its anti-PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody, RC148. This move positions AbbVie in the expanding field of bispecific antibodies targeting both PD-1 and VEGF pathways. The article highlights AbbVie's strategic decision to acquire this asset amidst increasing competition and development in this therapeutic area.
